element_introduction_order.mastery_context: core puzzle mechanic that creates difficulty

typical_tension_triggers: selecting tiles with constrained free edges
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Self-paced puzzle with tension building through board state complexity and dwindling move options

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low difficulty with abundant legal moves and obvious matches
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty as board complexity increases and move options narrow
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty with constrained move sets and endgame dead-lock risk
notable_spikes: Mid-game board state complexity increases exponentially
Late-game single-instance tiles (seasons/flowers) become blocking obstacles
Endgame scenarios where board becomes unwinnable if wrong sequence chosen

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard mahjong set (144 tiles total) with 4 instances each of numbered suits + winds + dragons, and single instances of seasons and flowers. This creates predictable pairing potential but unique constraints on rarest tiles.
Only tiles with at least one free vertical edge may be matched. This is the core puzzle mechanic that creates spatial thinking and lookahead requirements.
Upon clearing all tiles, portion of screen collapses to reveal animated dragon blinking eye (or fire-breathing on some versions), providing completion feedback.

sources_used: wikipedia (Shanghai gameplay mechanics, tile set composition, sequel information)
